message:
beads: normalize three legacy beads to canonical schema

br sync --flush-only normalized four .beads/issues.jsonl entries
to the canonical schema:

1. frankenpandas-review-inf01 had a duplicate JSON object appended
   to its line (same id repeated with an empty assignee). Collapse
   to a single canonical record. The duplicate was a harmless
   write-twice from a prior sync race; the working record was
   already correct.

2. frankenpandas-expr01, frankenpandas-fuzz01, frankenpandas-hll01
   used the older ad-hoc shape (priority/status/closed_at/
   closed_by/resolution/title/comment) instead of the canonical
   shape that every other bead in this file uses
   (title/description/status/priority/issue_type/assignee/
   created_at/created_by/updated_at/closed_at/close_reason/
   source_repo/compaction_level/original_size). Re-emit each in
   the canonical shape — the freeform `comment` field maps to
   `description`, `resolution` maps to `close_reason`, and the
   missing `created_at` / `updated_at` / `issue_type` are filled
   in from each bead's commit history.

All four beads keep status=closed and the same close_reason text,
so this is a pure schema migration with no information loss. The
referenced commits (985fe07, 06ab9fb, 2497512, cb3f2d5) remain
discoverable through the close_reason text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
